entries.definition:
كتاب القاف

قزع

(قسط) القاف والسين والطاء أصلٌ صحيح يدلُّ على معنَيَين متضادَّين والبناءُ واحد. فالقِسط: العَدل. ويقال منه أقْسَطَ يُقْسِط. قال الله تعالى:
priests and monks and they are not given to arrogance.
entries.definition_english: priests and monks and they are not given to arrogance.

entries.definition:
q–s–ṭ justice, equity, to do justice, to deny justice; balance, measure, share; instalment, to pay by instalments. Of this root, five forms occur 25 times in the Qur’an: تُقْسِط tuqsiṭ three times; قَاسِطُون qāsiṭūn twice; أَقْسَط ʾaqsaṭ twice; مُقْسِطِين muqsiṭīn three times and قِسْط qisṭ 15 times.
تُقْسِط tuqsiṭ [imperf. of v. IV أَقْسَط ʾaqsaṭa, intrans.] to act justly (4:3) وَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلَّا تُقْسِطُوا فِي الْيَتَامَى فَانْكِحُوا مَا طَابَ لَكُمْ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ and if you fear that you cannot deal justly by the orphans [in marrying them], then marry those women who seem agreeable to you.

قَاسِطُون qāsiṭūn [pl. of act. part. قَاسِط qāsiṭ] those acting unjustly, or unrighteously; those who deviate from the right path (72:14) وَأَنَّا مِنَّا الْمُسْلِمُونَ وَمِنَّا الْقَاسِطُونَ فَمَنْ أَسْلَمَ فَأُولَٰئِكَ تَحَرَّوْا رَشَدًا and among us there are the ones who surrender, and the ones who deviate: those who surrender have endeavoured to find guidance.

أَقْسَط ʾaqsaṭ [elat.] more/most just (33:5) ادْعُوهُمْ لِآبَائِهِمْ هُوَ أَقْسَطُ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ name them after their [real] fathers, this is more equitable with God.

مُقْسِطُون muqsiṭūn [pl. of act. part. مُقْسِط muqsiṭ] those who act justly or fairly, those who are even-handed (49:9) فَإِنْ فَاءَتْ فَأَصْلِحُوا بَيْنَهُمَا بِالْعَدْلِ وَأَقْسِطُوا إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُقْسِطِينَ then, if it [the aggressing party] returns, put things right between them equitably, and be just, surely God loves the just.

قِسْط qisṭ I [n.] justice (7:29) قُلْ أَمَرَ رَبِّي بِالْقِسْطِ say, ‘My Lord commands justice’ II [quasi-act. part.] fair, just (21:47) وَنَضَعُ الْمَوَازِينَ الْقِسْطَ لِيَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ and We set the scales, the just ones, for the Day of Resurrection.

قِسْطَاس qisṭās this word is regarded by early philologists as a borrowing from Greek meaning either ‘justice’ or ‘balance’. Other scholars, however, argue that قِسْطَاس qisṭās is a borrowing from either Aramaic or Syriac. قِسْطَاس qisṭās occurs twice in the Qur’an.

قِسْطَاس qisṭās [n.] balance, scales (17:35) وَأَوْفُوا الْكَيْلَ إِذَا كِلْتُمْ وَزِنُوا

757
